title: Objects, Scope, Linkage, Storage Duration, and Lifetime · C Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A safety board presents unfamiliar objects, scope, linkage, storage duration, and lifetime decisions across code, diagrams, diagnostics, and stakeholder evidence; each distractor corresponds to a named misconception. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for object lifetime and address ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish an object, its value, representation bytes, address, declared type, and current lifetime.
Map scope, linkage, and storage duration independently for block, file, static, thread, and allocated objects.
Identify precisely when each object lifetime begins and ends, including allocated storage and subobjects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
